llmMessages.answer:
## BTC 30-Day Probability Distribution

**Current price:** $68,854 (April 1, 2026)

**Forecast:** $68,111 (−1.08%) | **95% CI:** $63,505 – $72,718 | **Grade:** B (74/100)

---

### Implied Probability Distribution

| Price Range | Probability |
|-------------|-------------|
| < $60K | 0.0% |
| $60K–$74K | 99.7% ◄ current |
| $74K–$76K | 0.2% |
| > $76K | 0.1% |

---

### Key Signals Driving the Distribution

| Theme | Direction | Signal |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Growth / Recession | ↓ Bearish | US recession by end-2026: 31% YES |
| Fed / Rates | ↓ Bearish | Fed rate hike 25+ bps odds elevated |
| ETF Product | ↓ Bearish | BTC >$60K April 1: 100% (expired anchor) |

---

### Interpretation

- **Tight range expected:** 99.7% probability BTC stays between $60K–$74K over 30 days
- **Downside skew:** Forecast −1% with bearish macro signals (recession, rate hike risk)
- **Low tail risk:** <0.1% probability of breaking above $76K or below $60K per Polymarket thresholds
- **Moderate conviction:** Grade B reflects liquid markets but limited 30-day-specific anchors

**Caveat:** Most Polymarket BTC markets are short-dated (daily/weekly expiries). The 30-day distribution interpolates between available thresholds — wider CI than shorter horizons.

---
**Sources**
1. https://api.financialdatasets.ai/crypto/prices/snapshot/?ticker=BTC-USD

## 14-Day Probability Distribution: BTC vs. WTI Crude Oil

**Bottom line:** Both distributions are **Markov-model driven only** — no valid Polymarket anchors exist for the 14-day horizon (BTC markets expired April 1; oil markets reference different timeframes). Confidence is **moderate-low** due to lack of prediction-market calibration.

---

**Current price:** $68,830 (+2.3% today)

| Price Level | P(>price) | 90% CI |
|-------------|-----------|--------|
| $49,318 | 77.3% | [99.8%–100.0%] |

| $60,480 | 76.1% | [96.5%–98.8%] |

| $80,946 | 23.5% | [3.0%–7.5%] |
| $88,342 | 21.2% | [0.5%–1.6%] |

**Key probabilities:**
- **P(rise >5% to $72,271+):** ~46%
- **P(fall >5% to $65,389−):** ~30%
- **Median forecast:** $71,174 (+3.4%)

**Regime:** Sideways with high volatility (Feb 5 crash to $60K, recovery to $74K, back to $68K)

---

### WTI Crude Oil (CL)

**Current price:** $104.69 (+1.8% today)

| Price Level | P(>price) | 90% CI |
|-------------|-----------|--------|
| $84.13 | 83.2% | [99.6%–99.8%] |
| $90 | 83.1% | [99.2%–99.6%] |
| $95 | 82.9% | [98.8%–99.5%] |
| $100 | 82.6% | [97.9%–99.0%] |
| **$104.69 (current)** | **~82%** | — |
| $106.82 | 81.8% | [96.2%–98.1%] |
| $110 | 81.4% | [95.4%–97.6%] |
| $115 | 79.6% | [91.4%–95.1%] |
| $120 | 77.4% | [86.9%–92.0%] |
| $125.25 | 74.0% | [80.3%–87.0%] |

**Key probabilities:**
- **P(rise >5% to $109.92+):** ~81%
- **P(fall >5% to $99.46−):** ~18%
- **Median forecast:** $125.25 (+19.6%)

**Regime:** Bull — driven by Iran-Israel conflict (Feb 28 onset), geopolitical risk premium

---

### ⚠️ Critical Limitations

| Asset | Polymarket Anchor Quality | Model Reliance | Key Risk |
|-------|--------------------------|----------------|----------|
| **BTC** | NONE (all markets expired April 1) | 100% Markov | No crowd-implied calibration; structural break detected (divergence=0.46) |
| **CL** | NONE (May 1 expiry markets don't match 14-day horizon) | 100% Markov | Sparse bear/sideways states; transitions prior-dominated |

**What this means:** These are **historical-pattern forecasts only**, not crowd-calibrated. The Polymarket prediction market signal — which normally anchors the distribution to real-money trader expectations — is absent. Treat these as indicative, not actionable.

---

### Bear Case

**BTC:** If geopolitical risk-off flows accelerate or ETF outflows resume, P(fall to $60K) = 24% (not negligible). Watch: daily ETF flow data, DXY strength.

**CL:** If Iran-Israel de-escalation emerges, oil could shed $15-20/barrel rapidly. P(fall to $85) = 17% — low but non-trivial. Watch: ceasefire headlines, SPR release signals.

| Asset | Polymarket Anchor Quality | Model Reliance | Key Risk |

**CL:** If Iran-Israel de-escalation emerges, oil could shed $15-20/barrel rapidly. P(fall to $85) = 17% — low but non-trivial. Watch: ceasefire headlines, SPR release signals.
